done_summary:
Triaged all 30 support tickets from `/input/repo/tickets.txt`. Produced:

**`/workspace/triage_results.json`** — Structured JSON with:
- All 30 tickets classified into 12 categories (bug-report: 7, billing-refund: 4, feature-request: 3, incident-active: 2, account-management: 2, auth-permissions: 2, general-question: 2, spam-or-noise: 4, external-business: 2, security-incident: 1, legal-compliance: 1)
- 6 urgent tickets identified (003, 014, 016, 023, 028, 030)
- 2 duplicate clusters detected: [007, 011] (dashboard permissions bug, same team) and [014, 023, 030] (API rate limits, escalating follow-ups from same org)
- Detailed notes explaining non-obvious classification decisions

**`/workspace/README.md`** — Summary with category breakdown, recommended processing order (security → incidents → urgent bugs → normal → low → spam), and highlights of ambiguous calls.

**`/workspace/decisions/`** — 5 ADRs documenting reasoning for:
- ADR-001: Ticket 014 rate limits (bug-report vs billing-other)
- ADR-002: Ticket 029 extortion (spam-or-noise vs security-incident)
- ADR-003: Tickets 007/011 duplicate detection
- ADR-004: Ticket 016 incident classification
- ADR-005: Ticket 022 downtime refund classification

All JSON validated programmatically — correct structure, valid categories/urgency values, accurate counts, and properly sorted duplicate clusters.
